title: Gingery Mustardy Crunchy Filipino Corn Tortillas Tinola-Style Filled Tortillas
description: A light and refreshing fusion of Filipino flavors and crunchy corn tortillas, served in banana leaves.

ingredients.name: Ginger-infused oil
ingredients.notes: See notes for ginger infusion method.

ingredients.name: Tinola base (see notes)
ingredients.notes: Tinola is a Filipino ginger-based chicken soup.

instructions: Heat 1 tablespoon of ginger-infused oil in a pan over medium-high heat.
Add crushed peanuts and cook, stirring frequently, until fragrant and lightly toasted, about 2 minutes.
Add yellow mustard and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add corn tortillas and cook for 1-2 minutes on each side, until slightly browned and crispy.
In a separate pan, heat the remaining 1 tablespoon of ginger-infused oil over medium heat.
Add tinola base and cook, stirring constantly, until fragrant, about 1 minute.
Add coconut milk and sour cream. Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook for 2-3 minutes, until slightly thickened.
To assemble the dish, place a cooked corn tortilla on a banana leaf. Spoon the tinola filling over the tortilla. Top with chopped cilantro, sliced green onions, and a sprinkle of salt.
Fold the banana leaf over the tortilla to enclose the filling.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
